behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff from classifier module to API client payload contract (cross-module). Unit could miss wiring where builder output is not what dispatcher sends. Rejected System because behavior text only requires payload contents, not real network/OS/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no dependence on model judgment or multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client and assert the outbound request payload includes every configured taxonomy label on dispatch.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans API response handoff to module output mapping. A pure unit on normalization logic may miss integration mistakes in response parsing + mapping path. Rejected Agentic because test input is a controlled invalid label (no real model quality dependence). Rejected System because no real HTTP/OS is required to reproduce this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API response returning an out-of-taxonomy label through the normal module call path; assert caller receives "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly about one local normalization step determinism for identical inputs, with no required module boundary or I/O. Rejected Integration because behavior text scopes to normalization function outcome, not inter-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency tied to provider output; correctness depends on real model behavior/non-determinism. Mocked responses would hide this class of failure. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with distinct model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run multiple real API classifications for identical input/taxonomy, record raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label stability whenever provider response content is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is budget enforcement gating dispatch (constructor + dispatcher interaction). Unit on tokenizer logic alone could miss accidental dispatch despite over-budget input. Rejected System because enforcement is pre-dispatch and can be validated in-process. Rejected Agentic because no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: With module wired to a stub client, pass documents around budget thresholds and assert truncate/reject policy is applied and dispatch behavior matches policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires real provider-reported token usage from an external AI API call; replacing with stubs can hide real token accounting mismatches at the model provider boundary. Rejected Integration because the assertion depends on real AI API return fields, not only local routing/retry plumbing. Rejected Workflow because only one model call step is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents and assert provider-reported usage fields are <= configured budget per call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This tests orchestration between API adapter response handling, backoff/wait policy, and retry logic across modules. It does not require real network/OS if rate-limit responses and timing are simulated deterministically. Rejected System because real HTTP conditions are not necessary to trigger/verify the retry state transition. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on model output content or multi-agent decision chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API that returns rate-limit signal then success; verify module waits per policy and retries, ultimately returning successful classification instead of propagating immediate error.
